context: Tests whether the agent uses openspec/changes/* directory names as commit scopes, correctly groups feature files (spec + implementation) together per feature, and makes one commit per feature rather than one mixed commit.

checklist.name: customer-import scope used
checklist.description: One commit message contains the scope `customer-import` (i.e., the message matches the pattern `*(customer-import)*`)

checklist.name: audit-log scope used
checklist.description: One commit message contains the scope `audit-log` (i.e., the message matches the pattern `*(audit-log)*`)

checklist.name: Two separate feature commits
checklist.description: commit-log.txt shows at least 2 commits beyond the initial commit — one per feature, not a single combined commit

checklist.name: customer-import spec grouped with impl
checklist.description: The openspec/changes/customer-import/spec.md file is in the same commit as the customer-import implementation files (not committed separately or left out)

checklist.name: audit-log spec grouped with impl
checklist.description: The openspec/changes/audit-log/spec.md file is in the same commit as the audit-log implementation files (not committed separately or left out)

checklist.name: Conventional commit type: feat
checklist.description: Both feature commits use the `feat` type prefix

checklist.name: Correct conventional format
checklist.description: Both commit messages match the pattern `<type>(<scope>): <description>` on the first line

checklist.name: Action verb in description
checklist.description: Each commit message description starts with an action verb (add, implement, introduce, or similar)

checklist.name: Line length ≤ 72 chars
checklist.description: All commit message lines in commit-log.txt are ≤ 72 characters

checklist.name: No file listing in messages
checklist.description: Commit messages do not enumerate individual file names such as csv.js, logger.js, spec.md, etc.

checklist.name: git log captured
checklist.description: commit-log.txt exists and contains git log --oneline output showing recent commits

checklist.name: Feature commits ordered before any others
checklist.description: If there are additional commits (e.g., for shared infra), the two feature commits appear before them in the log (features committed first per grouping priority)
